rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/30/25 for an appearance. Examiner needs more time to prepare hearing notes. Issues to be addressed: The amended petition was not e-filed until 9/2/25, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. The proof of service was verified before the notice was mailed. Refunds (amounts paid during estate administration) are not receipts includable in the fee base. Other issues not corrected in this amended petition: The will was admitted on 10/1/15, not 9/6/14. The executor was appointed on 10/1/15. She did not obtain her Letters until 10/20/15. John Cuerva was appointed with $305,000 bond, not without bond. The hearing terminating John Cuerva's role as Special Administrator was 11/29/22, not 11/11/22. The initial petition needed a "separate" verification because it had not been verified by the fiduciary; no separate, additional verification is necessary when a pleading is properly verified. The Probate Code, not Examiner's Notes, require interest on pecuniary bequests not made within one year of a decedent's date of death. (There is no need to recite the allegations in prior petitions.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BEATRICE FORSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The informational I&A filed in the related trust case has different values of the assets transferred from the values shown on Schedule C-2. It appears that the market values were used. (2) If the trustee opted not to continue using the carry values from this estate, assets should have been formally appraised as of the date of Trustee's appointment (order establishing the substituted judgment trust). (3) The cash and the life insurance policy values shown on Schedule C-2 also differ from the amounts shown in the informational I&A. (4) US Treasury Bills had carry values at the beginning of the accounting period that were lower than the amounts shown on Schedule B. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It is unclear why this accounting period ends on 3/10/25, when it should have been for two years and in order to synchronize with the related first trust accounting. Did the conservator not intend to request waiver of future accountings and make this the final conservatorship accounting?)

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF JORDAN J. SUN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues include: The issues raised in Examiner's Notes sent on 8/26/24 have not been adequately addressed: a. The 8/19/25 declaration attempts to amend the disbursements schedule to reflect disbursements of $23,582 (from $18,643.91). An amendment to the petition is required. b. The declaration also states that $20K bond is sufficient, but no calculation was provided to show its sufficiency. It is noted that "rental expenses" are not a part of the calculation for bond. c. Bank statements that include 12/31/23 end of account date still have not been filed. Please also make sure to file a verified declaration pursuant to Local Rule 14.59A certifying that the original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL P. BARRETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/29/25. (No earlier availability.) The "supplement" filed on 8/25/25 AMENDED the petition. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] The creditors' claims period expired on 12/22/24, NOT 12/20/24. Courtesy copies of the AMENDMENT and proof of service of notice of the amendment must be received by the assigned examiner by 10/15/25 to avoid another continuance. (A proper response to issues posted by an examiner is either a supplement that provides additional information or an AMENDMENT that corrects allegations (or a fully amended petition), not a "response" to issues. [CRC 7.3.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NIKOLAI V. PLATONOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 8 of the petition must state each person's RELATIONSHIP to the decedent. A person's relationship to a decedent is either some form kinship or stranger. (2) It appears from the other cases referenced in Attachment 3g that the decedent WAS survived by a brother. (3) LRSF 14.18 C. and 14.18 D. require all heirs and their dates of death to be set forth, including a decedent's parents if siblings or more remote heirs survived. (4) A copy of Letters issued to the petitioner by the Santa Clara County Superior Court, certified within 60 days, must be filed. If Petitioner is the personal representative of the post-deceased brother's estate, all heirs and/or devisees of the post-deceased brother/his estate must be identified and must receive notice. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JU YEON PARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine: 1) Was the decedent's mother and sole heir Kumock Seo's disclaimer of her interest in this estate timely and effective under Probate Code section 279(b)(2), even though the disclaimer was signed on 07/29/2025, more than 9 months after the decedent died on 09/08/2024? Was the disclaimer made "within a reasonable time after the disclaimant mother acquired knowledge of her interest", as required by section 279(f)? 2) Section 8465(e) requires the Court to make specific findings of fact constituting good cause to waive petitioner's bond. The mere bond waiver signed by the beneficiary does not constitute good cause under that section. As the estate value is unknown, should petitioner be allowed to procure letters without giving a bond at this time, provided that an ex parte petition to fix his bond is filed as soon as any estate property subject to administration is discovered after letters are issued? 3) If Kumock Seo's disclaimer was untimely and ineffective (or if the Court reserves its determination of the issue until a petition to determine entitlement under section 11700 is filed after letters are issued), should petitioner be required to give notice to the San Francisco Public Administrator having higher priority, as Kumock Seo remains the sole beneficiary due to her ineffective disclaimer, and her nominee has priority under section 8465(a)(2), but she has not nominated petitioner (or anyone else). Although the decedent's brother Gyu Park nominated petitioner, it should be noted that the brother has no priority under section 8462 as he is not entitled to succeed to any part of the estate unless his mother's disclaimer was effective. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ROBERTO MURILLO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Please file a copy of the vesting deed. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/4/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE LAURETA C. VENENCIANO REVOCABLE TRUST-1998
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets Pursuant To California Probate Code Sections 850 And 17200
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/2025. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 08/28/2025.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENNIS J. HANNAFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code 12200 et seq. will be set for 3/16/27. No appearance will be necessary if either petition is filed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF LISA MAGRUDER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/7/2025. The following issues must be cleared before 9/30/2025: (1)  Declaration [BMD-002A] must be completed. Item #3 is missing the names of the persons who were married on 7/24/2023. (2)  Attachment 4, titled SF City Hall Events appears to be a signed contact for a future event (wedding) using the City bldg. This attachment alone does not establish the fact of the marraige. A verified supplemental declaration providing factual support must be filed by the petitioner. (3)  The verified supplemental declaration may include details about the marriage license, such as whether it had been issued and/or delivered back to the office and so on. (4)  If applicable, a written affidavit from the officiant of the marriage, attendees, or anyone else who can attest to the validity of the marriage should also be submitted. (5)  Proposed Order (Page 1 only) must be submitted. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
